text.title: Python Type Hints Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end example: untyped FastAPI handler module migrated to PEP 604 + Protocol + TypedDict + mypy --strict gate green.

section.title: Before — untyped
After — typed (Python 3.12, PEP 604, TypedDict)
section.note: Pydantic guards the HTTP boundary; TypedDict types the internal response shape. No mixed Optional/Union syntax. Return type explicit on every signature.

section.title: mypy --strict run
section.code:
$ mypy --strict src/api/users.py Success: no issues found in 1 source file
$ ruff check --select UP007 src/api/users.py All checks passed!

section.title: CI gate trace
section.p: PR touches `src/api/users.py`. `typecheck-touched.sh main` runs `git diff --name-only main -- '*.py'` → `src/api/users.py`. `mypy --strict src/api/users.py` exits 0. PR check turns green. Append `src/api/users.py` to `[tool.mypy] files` to lock the file as strict-typed going forward.

text.reference: CI script used in this run
